summary: Iteration 73: Verified notification click navigation feature. All 6 notification types tested successfully navigate to correct routes. Backend API tests pass (13/13), frontend Playwright tests pass (6/6 navigation tests + mark all read).
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/notifications returns list sorted by created_at desc
Backend: GET /api/notifications/unread-count returns integer count
Backend: PUT /api/notifications/{id}/read marks single notification as read
Backend: PUT /api/notifications/read-all marks all as read (count becomes 0)
Backend: Notification endpoints require authentication (401 without token)
Backend: NEW_FOLLOWER notification has follower_username in data
Backend: POST_LIKED notification has post_id in data
Backend: TRADE_PROPOSED notification has trade_id in data
Backend: STRIPE_CONNECTED notification has correct structure
Backend: ORDER_SHIPPED notification has order_id in data
Backend: WAX_REPORT notification has correct structure
Frontend: NEW_FOLLOWER notification click → navigates to /profile/{follower_username}
Frontend: POST_LIKED notification click → navigates to /hive
Frontend: TRADE_PROPOSED notification click → navigates to /trades
Frontend: STRIPE_CONNECTED notification click → navigates to /settings
Frontend: ORDER_SHIPPED notification click → navigates to /orders
Frontend: WAX_REPORT notification click → navigates to /wax-reports
Frontend: Notification dropdown shows correct icons for each type
Frontend: Mark all read button works (sets all to read, badge disappears)
Frontend: Clicking notification marks it as read (unread count decreases)

notification_click_navigation.description: handleClick function in NotificationBell routes each notification type to correct destination

notification_icons.description: NOTIF_ICONS map provides correct emoji icons for each notification type

mark_all_read.description: Mark all read button sets all notifications to read

mark_single_read.description: Clicking notification marks it as read

critical_code_review_comments: handleClick function (Navbar.js:380-412) correctly routes all notification types to appropriate destinations
Data-driven routing: checks data fields (trade_id, follower_username, order_id, post_id, listing_id) first, then falls back to type-based routing
NOTIF_ICONS map (Navbar.js:414-423) covers all documented notification types with appropriate emojis
Async mark-read call uses .catch(() => {}) to prevent navigation blocking on API failure - acceptable pattern for UX
seed_data_creation: Created 6 test notifications (NEW_FOLLOWER, POST_LIKED, TRADE_PROPOSED, STRIPE_CONNECTED, ORDER_SHIPPED, WAX_REPORT) for testnotif73 user
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 73 verified the notification click navigation feature. The handleClick function in NotificationBell component (Navbar.js) now routes each notification type to its correct destination based on data fields (trade_id→/trades, follower_username→/profile/{username}, order_id→/orders, post_id→/hive, listing_id→/honeypot/listing/{id}) or type (WAX_REPORT→/wax-reports, STRIPE_CONNECTED→/settings, BINGO→/nectar, MOOD_BOARD→/profile/{username}, streak_nudge→/hive, PRICE_ALERT→/honeypot). Test user testnotif73@test.com can be used for notification testing.
